slides.title: What is an RNN?
slides.content: Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) are a class of neural networks that are particularly effective for sequence data and time series. They are designed to recognize patterns in sequences of data, such as text, handwriting, or the temporal sequence in videos.

slides.title: RNN Architecture
slides.content: RNNs use loops to allow information to persist. This is crucial for processing sequences of data where past information is relevant.

slides.title: Benefits of RNNs
slides.content:
1. Ability to store state across sequences. 
2. Useful for tasks involving temporal sequences.
3. Can handle variable-length inputs.

slides.title: RNN Limitations
slides.content: RNNs struggle with long-term dependencies due to vanishing gradient problems.

visual_assets.content: \frac{\partial L}{\partial W} = \frac{\partial L}{\partial o} \cdot \frac{\partial o}{\partial h} \cdot \cdots \cdot \frac{\partial h}{\partial W}
visual_assets.caption: Gradient Calculation in RNNs

slides.title: Applications of RNNs
slides.content: RNNs are used in a variety of applications such as natural language processing, speech recognition, and time series forecasting.

slides.title: Example Code
slides.content: A simple RNN implementation in Python.

visual_assets.code:
import torch.nn as nn

rnn = nn.RNN(input_size=10, hidden_size=20, num_layers=2)
input = torch.randn(5, 3, 10)
h0 = torch.randn(2, 3, 20)
output, hn = rnn(input, h0)
visual_assets.caption: Simple RNN Example

slides.title: Conclusion
slides.content: RNNs are a powerful family of neural networks suitable for time-series and natural language data. Despite their limitations, improvements like LSTMs and GRUs have enhanced their capabilities.
